hypothesis: A coordinated heterogeneous ensemble of LLM agents—differing in sampling temperature and initial seeds and each given disjoint experimental budgets and overseen by a supervisor that aggregates summaries and redirects experiments toward under-sampled regions—will, under a fixed total experimental time budget, discover a higher fraction of the ground-truth reaction rules and achieve greater coverage of the reactor experimental state space (measured by UMAP occupancy area and unique labeled-state visits) than any single-agent baseline using the same total budget.
